description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, alternating between raised stone and recessed channels. The block is solid and has a uniform, dark coloration with a slightly marbled texture visible on the stone surfaces. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, contrasting with the textured top. The overall appearance suggests durability and a utilitarian design, likely intended to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. The block's robust construction and grooved surface may provide additional grip or drainage.
description: A solid rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The block is dark, marbled, and built for durability in urban settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the stripes running vertically from this perspective.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the same grooved pattern across the top surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view, again emphasizing the vertical grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared gravel base
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears clean with a consistent marbled texture.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
